title: Creating custom roles allowing privilege escalation is security-sensitive
description: Custom roles in cloud environments can be misconfigured to allow privilege escalation, enabling users to gain unauthorized access or perform actions beyond their intended scope. Privilege escalation is a critical security risk that can lead to full environment compromise.
message: Custom role allows privilege escalation. Restrict permissions to least privilege.
impact: Privilege escalation can result in unauthorized access, data breaches, and loss of control over cloud resources.
recommendation: Review custom roles for permissions that enable privilege escalation (e.g., iam.roles.create, serviceAccount.iamAdmin). Grant only the minimum permissions required.

noncompliant.description: Role grants permissions that can be used for privilege escalation.

compliant.description: Role grants only read-only permissions, preventing privilege escalation.

devsecops.security: Restricting permissions prevents attackers from escalating privileges and compromising the environment.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD should scan for risky permissions in custom roles.
devsecops.compliance: Least privilege is a core requirement of most security frameworks (e.g., NIST, CIS).

why_is_this_an_issue: Custom roles with excessive permissions can be abused for privilege escalation, leading to security breaches.
how_to_fix: Audit all custom roles. Remove or restrict permissions that allow privilege escalation. Enforce least privilege and review roles regularly.
